findings.description: emitEvent() could be used for JS injection if event name is attacker-controlled
findings.details: Added allowlist of known EIP-1193 event names (chainChanged, accountsChanged, connect, disconnect)

findings.description: sendError() message escaping did not handle newline characters which could break JS string
findings.details: Added \n and \r escaping to prevent JS string literal breakout

findings.description: WebView security hardening matches Decision 9 requirements: allowFileAccess=false, mixedContentMode=MIXED_CONTENT_NEVER_ALLOW, geolocationEnabled=false, javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically=false
findings.details: All required security settings present and correct

findings.description: Origin validation correctly extracts scheme+host+port, handles default ports, validates on every bridge call
findings.details: Proper origin comparison prevents cross-origin attacks

findings.description: URL validation blocks all dangerous schemes (file, javascript, data, http) and private IP ranges
eth_sign correctly rejected with explicit error message
findings.details: Follows tech-spec: deprecated and unsafe method is blocked

summary: Security hardening is thorough and matches Decision 9 requirements. Two JS injection vectors in EthereumBridge fixed (event name allowlist, error message newline escaping). No remaining security vulnerabilities.
